subject: Anthropology
title: Speech Communities
description: Learn Speech Communities in one minute.

hook.text: A group of skaters has its own slang. So does Wall Street. Each group builds a shared way of talking.

buildup.text: A speech community is a group that shares linguistic norms, vocabulary, and ways of using language.

discovery.text: Membership isn't about where you live but who you interact with. Online communities create new speech norms too.

twist.text: Everyone belongs to multiple speech communities simultaneously — family, work, friends — and shifts between them.

climax.text: Speech communities show that language is social glue. Shared ways of talking create belonging and identity.

quiz.question: What defines a speech community?
options: Sharing linguistic norms and ways of using language
